data.current_summary: Execute a GROQ query via GET
data.description_suggestion: Executes a GROQ query against the specified dataset using HTTP GET method. Suitable for queries under 11KB. Accepts query parameters including the GROQ query string, optional parameters for variable substitution, and caching preferences. Returns a QueryResponse object containing the matched documents and query metadata. Requires appropriate dataset read permissions.

data.current_summary: Execute a GROQ query via POST (for queries exceeding 11KB URL limit)
data.description_suggestion: Executes a GROQ query against the specified dataset using HTTP POST method. Recommended for queries exceeding 11KB or containing complex parameters. Accepts a QueryRequest body containing the GROQ query string and optional parameters for variable substitution. Returns a QueryResponse object containing the matched documents and query metadata. Supports all GROQ query features without URL length constraints. Requires appropriate dataset read permissions.

data.current_summary: Apply mutations to documents in a dataset
data.description_suggestion: Applies one or more mutations to documents in the specified dataset. Accepts a MutationRequest body containing mutation operations (create, update, patch, delete, createIfNotExists). Mutations are atomic - all succeed or all fail. Returns a MutationResponse containing the results of each mutation operation, including created/modified document IDs and any errors. Modifies document state in the dataset. Requires appropriate dataset write permissions and valid API token authentication.

data.current_summary: Get documents by ID, bypassing caching
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves one or more documents from the specified dataset by their document IDs, bypassing any CDN or edge caching layers. Accepts comma-separated document IDs in the path parameter. Returns the full document objects including all fields and metadata. Useful for retrieving the latest document state when cache freshness is critical. Requires appropriate dataset read permissions. Returns 404 if any requested document does not exist.
